metadata.title: Knapsack's Hidden Exponential: One Zero, Ten Times Slower

beats.narration_text: Jambo. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of knapsack's hidden exponential: one zero, ten times slower. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of knapsack's hidden exponential: one zero, ten times slower
output: ✓ Knapsack's Hidden Exponential: One Zero, Ten Times Slower

beats.narration_text: Knapsack's Hidden Exponential: One Zero, Ten Times Slower is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. the core behaviour of knapsack's hidden exponential: one zero, ten times slower. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
beats.new_visual_element: Concept card: Knapsack's Hidden Exponential: One Zero, Ten Times Slower

beats.narration_text: Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Knapsack's Hidden Exponential: One Zero, Ten Times Slower. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: d-slider. Verify: the key behaviour of knapsack's hidden exponential: one zero, ten times slower. Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Knapsack's Hidden Exponential: One Zero, Ten Times Slower. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: d-slider. Ver

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.
beats.code_snippet: // Knapsack's Hidden Exponential: One Zero, Ten Times Slower — core physics
beats.new_visual_element: Onda code-block: core JS (integrator + slider handler)
beats.prompt: Show the core physics loop from knapsack-pseudopolynomial.html

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: knapsack's hidden exponential: one zero, ten times slower. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag main slider from 1 to 8. Watch the physics respond in real time.
beats.new_visual_element: Slate: narrate slider sweep of main slider from 1 to 8

beats.narration_text: the prediction holds — knapsack's hidden exponential: one zero, ten times slower behaves as the equations say. Exactly as the equations predict.

One prompt. A live demonstration of knapsack's hidden exponential: one zero, ten times slower. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this Knapsack's Hidden Exponential: One Zero, Ten Times Slower simulation and add a second slider that controls the main parameter. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.
